commands.author: clawSean

target.title: fix(telegram): route poll answers into sessions
target.body:
Fixes #89573.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users could vote in a public/non-anonymous Telegram poll sent by OpenClaw, but the vote would never reach the originating agent session.

Telegram sends `poll_answer` updates only for non-anonymous polls, and those updates do not include chat or thread routing metadata. Current main can send polls and already requests/receives the relevant update type, but without a `poll_answer` routing path the user's selected option is effectively display-only.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the missing inbound half of Telegram poll support by recording the poll origin when a public poll is sent, then looking that origin up when Telegram later delivers a `poll_answer`.

The registry is stored in the shared plugin keyed-state store (`telegram.poll-registry`), not a JSON sidecar. Poll origin registration lives at the central `sendPollTelegram` boundary, so both the message action path and the outbound adapter path are covered by the same write.

The inbound handler follows the same routing/auth shape as other Telegram engagement events:

- unknown poll ids are ignored
- bot voters are ignored before chat I/O
- empty `option_ids` retractions are ignored before chat I/O
- existing Telegram sender authorization still runs before enqueueing
- DM `requireTopic=true` still blocks when no topic is known
- forum/topic routing uses the thread id resolved at send time
- store read/write failures are best-effort and do not crash the update handler or fail an already-sent poll

Anonymous poll behavior is unchanged. Telegram does not emit `poll_answer` for anonymous polls, so those remain display-only from the agent's perspective.

## User Impact

Public Telegram polls can now be used as one-tap agent input. When a user votes in a non-anonymous poll sent by OpenClaw, the selected option is routed back into the originating direct chat, group, or forum-topic session.

This does not add or change any config, environment variable, CLI option, default anonymity behavior, governance file, dependency manifest, or release/security review surface.

## Evidence

Current clean pushed candidate: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

Base used for current-head validation: `origin/main` at `2e6e17f7c5d3664a76d81d2c967bcf11051cefb3`

Focused local validation on the current head:

```console
env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/action-runtime.test.ts
# 5 files passed, 314 tests passed

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json
# exit 0

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# all matched files use the correct format

env PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules /root/projects/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxlint -c .oxlintrc.json --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/allowed-updates.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/poll-registry.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.ts
# exit 0

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# clean
```

Behavior covered by tests:

- poll registry stores, retrieves, misses safely, and remains bounded
- public poll send records registry context
- anonymous poll send does not record registry context
- topic-suffixed targets store the resolved `message_thread_id`
- registry write failure does not fail an already-sent poll
- `poll_answer` routes to the originating group/topic session
- authorized DM poll votes route to the DM session
- DM `requireTopic=true` blocks when no topic is known
- missing registry entries are ignored
- unauthorized senders are blocked
- bot voters and retractions are dropped before chat I/O
- registry lookup failure does not crash or enqueue

The earlier Foreman review on the same Telegram poll-answer diff found no implementation or policy blocker. This update rebased the same single-commit diff onto current `origin/main`; focused tests, extension typecheck, touched-file formatting, touched-file type-aware lint, and whitespace checks passed on the new head above.

Remaining proof gap:

- Live Telegram Desktop/Mantis proof is not included in this contributor-supplied update. The prior Mantis run on the old stale head failed to show the expected candidate vote-to-session result, and I cannot self-trigger maintainer-gated Mantis. This PR keeps the `mantis: telegram-visible-proof` context and asks maintainers to run the visible proof against the new pushed head.
- Final pushed PR branch SHA: `6b62999d9064add8eaccaee5612bd40fcccc559f`

## Related Work

- #89573 is the canonical open issue for this missing `poll_answer` route.
- #72501 was the original PR for this route. It was closed after the stale sidecar-era version failed proof, and GitHub will not let this contributor account reopen it after the maintainer/bot close. This PR is the current-main v2 replacement.
- #18122 merged Telegram poll send/action wiring, but did not complete the remaining `poll_answer` routing path. This PR preserves the access-control concerns raised around that prior work.
- #17357 was a broader stale poll attempt with unrelated scope. This PR is the narrow follow-up: no dependencies, no new skill, no CLI/API redesign, and no default-anonymity change.
- #95309 is a newer draft candidate for the same #89573 root cause. This PR's v2 takes the sidecar-free keyed-store approach and avoids branch-only JSON migration behavior.

## AI Assistance

Implementation and review were assisted by AI tooling. The final diff was reviewed against current OpenClaw source and validated with the commands above.
